observations.summary: Bartender at the Chatsubo with a conspicuous prosthetic arm.
A figure accompanied by two hired enforcers who may have business with Case.
A man whose sex workers frequent the Chatsubo.
Unable to enter cyberspace, he hustles in Night City and remains tormented by dreams of the matrix.
He is twenty-four and was formerly one of the Sprawl's best cyberspace thieves.
A legendary cyberspace professional who helped train Case.
A legendary cyberspace professional who helped train Case.
After stealing from his employers, he was caught and deliberately prevented from working again.
A Russian mycotoxin inflicted subtle but effective damage on his nervous system.
He came to Chiba seeking a medical cure for his neural damage.
After exhausting his money on unsuccessful treatment, he lives in cheap portside coffin lodgings.
During his first month in Chiba, he killed two men and one woman over small sums.
He deals recklessly, neglects precautions, and recognizes his own accelerating self-destruction.
A young woman Case first met while she was playing in a Ninsei arcade.
Her personality and behavior have been increasingly consumed by drug addiction.
She is twenty years old, from the southern Sprawl, and looks unhealthy beneath her eyes.
She warns Case that Wage may intend to have him killed.
Linda's source for the warning about Wage; her new partner works for him.
He owes Wage money but believes the debt is too small to justify killing him.
He gives Linda fifty New Yen despite his own financial danger.
She urges Case to find Wage and watch his back.
A long-established Chiba dealer in prohibited biological goods with extensive criminal connections.
He is 135 and uses expensive hormones, serums, and annual genetic surgery to resist aging.
An importer with antiquarian tastes and an office behind Ninsei.
He regards Wage as an important connection and is cautious about sharing information concerning him.
He knows of no plan by Wage to kill Case.
A slender, dark-haired person in mirrored glasses and dark clothes follows Case.
A boy working at a sushi stall who rents weapons.
He uses false identities for financial and travel documents.
The Chatsubo's resident pimp, he is addicted to hypnotics called Cloud Dancers.
A man who previously discussed a prohibited-hormone deal with Case and Wage.
He sets an ambush for his pursuer but flees through a window when fear overwhelms him.
The pursuer is a woman with a northern Sprawl accent.
He escapes with an injured left ankle.
He obtains a pistol and nine rounds from Shin and discards the cobra weapon.
He enters his rented coffin at Cheap Hotel and locks himself inside.
A Shinjuku business contact expecting Case to deliver illicit biological goods.
He asks Case to supply the goods on credit and ends the call when Case needs immediate payment.
A young Brazilian tending the Chatsubo bar before dawn.
He covers Wage and his enforcers with a riot gun to prevent violence in the Chatsubo.
He protects Case by confronting Wage's group and taking Case's pistol when Case aims it at Wage.
He has vat-grown green replacement eyes and travels with two augmented enforcers.
He denies planning to kill Case and suggests someone may be manipulating Case.
He points his rented pistol at Wage while demanding an explanation for the reported death threat.
His debt to Wage is settled by surrendering the flask of pituitaries.
She takes Case's Hitachi computer and leaves the hotel.
He learns that Linda took his Hitachi computer and left.
He returns Shin's pistol, recovers his deposit, and sells back the ammunition.
She injured an arcade security guard who pursued her with nunchucks.
She is not working for Wage and had not encountered Linda before reaching Case's hotel.
Her mirrored lenses are surgically inset and seal her eye sockets.
She has been sent by her employer to bring Case to a meeting unharmed.
She is capable of violence and has retractable scalpel blades beneath all ten fingernails.
A wealthy, physically imposing man who has Molly bring Case to his Hilton suite.
His earring identifies him to Case as Special Forces.
He claims to have participated in the Screaming Fist operation that pioneered modern intrusion programs.
Armitage's behavioral model predicts that Case is suicidal and likely to survive only another month on the street.
He offers to repair Case's neural damage in exchange for work under terms familiar to Case.
She has worked as Armitage's street samurai for roughly two months.
Before joining Armitage, she worked as a sex worker for someone else.
She does not know the true identity or nature of the power behind Armitage's operation.
Her optical implants provide night vision through microchannel image amplification.
His treatment includes a new pancreas, replacement liver tissue, altered blood and spinal fluid, and extensive neural injections.
An optic-nerve implant gives her a precise internal time readout.
He must wait eight days after surgery before safely testing whether he can access cyberspace again.
He and Molly become sexually intimate while she tends him after surgery.
He has a temporary arrangement with the Yakuza, who conceal information about him from Deane's network.
He spent the war profiting from markets in Lisbon rather than serving in the military.
He uses Armitage's money to pay Wage in cash before leaving Chiba.
His new pancreas and liver modifications make amphetamine and cocaine ineffective.
She shoots and kills a razor-wielding attacker before he can cut Case's throat.
Case finds Linda dead beside a pillar at Sammi's after a laser attack.
Deane's associates killed her and took Case's RAM when she tried to sell it.
His associates murdered Linda rather than pay her for Case's stolen RAM.
She interrogates and fatally wounds the laser attacker to learn who killed Linda and why.
After his postoperative check, he leaves Chiba with Molly and Armitage aboard a chartered hovercraft.
He charters a hovercraft and departs Chiba with Case and Molly.
He returns home to the Sprawl and stays with Molly in a factory loft.
He reveals that Case was implanted with fifteen slowly dissolving sacs containing the toxin that previously damaged him.
His continued neural health now depends on completing Armitage's job and receiving an enzyme treatment before the toxin sacs open.
He equips Case with an advanced Ono-Sendai deck, computer, monitor, and corporate-grade intrusion software.
A guarded technician operating a shielded scanning and privacy room behind Metro Holografix.
He is Molly's longtime connection, primarily a software fence, and has been installed as the operation's local technician.
The famed cyberspace operator known as the Dixie Flatline is dead after a fatal heart event.
Sense/Net paid to record his personality and retains the resulting construct in a secured library vault.
He is alive in Europe and is not part of the current operation.
She is secretly trying to identify the unknown power directing Armitage.
She tells Case their immediate mission is to steal Pauley's construct from Sense/Net while leaving other valuable material untouched.
Molly's investigation found no participant named Armitage in Screaming Fist and no resemblance between him and known survivors.
A prospective member of Armitage's operation whom Molly's profile identifies as a dangerous psychopath.
He accepts Molly's proposal that they cooperate privately within Armitage's operation.
He successfully returns to cyberspace for five hours and weeps with relief.
He begins modifying Case's deck with a protected prototype switch for accessing simstim without leaving cyberspace.
Finn is fitting her with a broadcast rig intended to make her sensory experience accessible through Case's deck.
A commercial simstim performer whose recorded experiences are edited to remove unwanted discomfort.
He tests the new one-way simstim link and experiences Molly's senses while remaining unable to control or answer her.
A software-booth attendant in Memory Lane who uses implanted microsofts.
He detects Case observing through Molly's eyes and serves as her contact for reaching the Panther Moderns.
An NYU sociologist who analyzes the Panther Moderns as self-aware products of the media environment rather than conventional terrorists.
A young Panther Modern with extensive elective facial and dental modifications.
He delivers a box of diskettes from Finn to Case's loft.
He spends nine days mapping and preparing a route through Sense/Net's defenses.
A Panther Modern communications coordinator using the operational alias Brood.
He coordinates Molly and Case during the raid, relays their messages, and arranges medical help for Molly.
She infiltrates the Sense/Net building and incapacitates a security guard in an elevator.
Her left leg is broken during a fight with Sense/Net security.
She suppresses the pain with endorphin analog and continues toward the construct despite her broken leg.
He supplies technical materials and a purchased command sequence used in the Sense/Net intrusion.
He penetrates Sense/Net's systems, disables and deceives its security records, and withdraws while concealing the construct's theft.
She physically removes Pauley's construct from its secured Sense/Net locker.
His recorded construct is successfully stolen from Sense/Net and carried away by Molly.
A Panther Modern helping Molly escape the chaotic Sense/Net lobby after her injury.
The pink-haired leader of the Panther Moderns, equipped with extensive implants and an advanced recording suit.
He reports that the Moderns have placed Molly with a medical team and will protect her.
Despite objecting to the raid's disorder, he pays Lupus Yonderboy for the Moderns' work.
She is undergoing surgery after the Sense/Net raid.
He has killed three people in Chiba, including two men and a woman.
An unknown entity identified as the source of a cryptic message for Case.
He finds Case after the raid and delivers the single-word message Wintermute.
He receives a cryptic message naming Wintermute, sent by an unknown source using Lupus as messenger.
She leaves post-raid treatment limping, with her injured leg still at risk of further damage.
He identifies Wintermute as an artificial intelligence registered in Berne and built for Tessier-Ashpool.
It is an artificial intelligence based in Berne whose mainframe and original software belong to Tessier-Ashpool.
She secretly paid Larry and the Panther Moderns to determine who controls Armitage.
He supplies the Moderns' finding that Wintermute is connected to the force behind Armitage.
An illicit fence who also operates as an art dealer and uses implanted art-market reference programs.
A burglar who returns from orbit with an ornate speaking computer terminal stolen from Villa Straylight.
A highly capable cloned assassin who penetrates Smith's security while seeking the stolen Tessier-Ashpool terminal.
He recovers the terminal, pays Smith's stated price, and obtains Jimmy's identity as its supplier.
He dies within days of Smith identifying him to the assassin.
After commissioning a concealed inquiry into Tessier-Ashpool, he abandons the investigation because of the risks.
His earlier investigation found Tessier-Ashpool to be an insular, cloning-dependent orbital family corporation controlling much of Freeside.
She gives Case purchased access details and directs him to investigate Armitage's London database.
He once survived brain death caused by defensive software, an achievement that made other elite operators shun him.
His eventual death resulted from a failing Russian replacement heart implanted during wartime captivity.
His ROM construct does not retain experiences after being disconnected unless supplied with external sequential memory.
With Pauley's construct assisting, he accesses and searches Armitage's London database.
His activated construct guides Case through communications access and into Armitage's unsecured database.
Armitage is identified as Colonel Willis Corto, the central figure in records concerning the Screaming Fist hearings.
He survived the failed Screaming Fist mission but reached military care blind, without legs, and missing most of his jaw.
A political operative who finds the severely injured Corto and recruits him as a witness in the Screaming Fist investigation.
After being surgically rebuilt and rehearsed, he gives largely fabricated testimony that protects officers responsible for suppressing warnings about Russian defenses.
He kills the Congressional aide after being warned against discussing the trials with others.
He is killed by Corto after threatening him into silence.
After the trials, he moves through espionage and organized crime while committing increasingly violent offenses.
He is later institutionalized with schizophrenia and catatonia, then becomes the sole successful patient in an experimental cybernetic treatment program.
He orders Case and Molly to depart for Istanbul that night.
A discreet dentist who operates a large clinic and repairs injured street samurai.
Her leg remains painful enough that she would prefer another night of treatment before traveling.
He travels toward the airport with Molly for the ordered trip to Istanbul.
Despite her injured leg, she travels toward the airport with Case for the Istanbul assignment.
He arrives in Istanbul with Molly for Armitage's operation.
He joins Case and Molly at their Istanbul hotel to examine Riviera's implants.
A young Armenian operative assigned to brief Case and Molly about Riviera.
He supplies Riviera's address, nightly route, and performance history to aid the team's capture plan.
His implants project imagined images into other people's vision and can deliver subliminal or tightly focused effects.
He has one enhanced lung after losing the other and depends on a cocaine-meperidine mixture.
When ambushed, he projects a grotesque creature emerging from his apparently ruined body and uses the illusion to conceal a gunshot.
Riviera shoots his hand during the ambush, costing him most of one middle finger.
A large Turkish associate who helps Finn remove the unconscious Riviera from the ambush site.
She incapacitates and captures Riviera with a toxin flechette, then ends Terzibashjian's involvement.
He is a corrupt secret-police torturer whom Armitage bought to assist the operation.
He tells Molly that Armitage is Corto and shares Corto's history with her.
She concludes with Case that Wintermute may have rebuilt Corto's personality into Armitage and now activates him as needed.
She wants Case to contact Wintermute and discover the purpose behind its operation.
He is sexually compelled to betray partners after winning their love.
Over three years he delivered eighteen young women to the secret police by manipulating them into political activity.
A bazaar dealer who supplies the drug mixture Riviera needs to function.
He again coerces Case by warning that the implanted toxin sacs are beginning to deteriorate.
After examining Riviera's implants, he departs Istanbul for home.
He arranges for himself, Case, Molly, and Riviera to travel from Istanbul toward Freeside.
He is legally stateless, travels on a forged Dutch passport, and comes from the radioactive ruins surrounding old Bonn.
After being captured and examined, he is pressured by Armitage into joining the team bound for Freeside.
It directly calls Case at the Istanbul Hilton and asks to speak with him.
He refuses Wintermute's direct telephone contact by hanging up.
After Case hangs up, it demonstrates continued access by ringing each pay phone once as he passes.
She strikes Riviera and threatens him into stopping his subliminal projections during the flight.
He travels from Istanbul through Paris and orbital transit to the Zion cluster.
His first orbital journey causes severe but temporary space adaptation syndrome.
A skinny Zionite who helps Case navigate the colony's freefall corridor.
He schedules eighty hours in Zion for zero-gravity practice, acclimatization, and a briefing on Freeside and Villa Straylight.
During the Zion stay he withdraws from the group while intoxicated and surrounds himself with projected geometric forms.
He practices operating in cyberspace while adapting to zero gravity in Zion.
His construct asks Case to erase it after the operation ends.
He briefly experiences Case's cyberspace interface and identifies what he sees with Babylon.
He theatrically projects a snake and scorpion while injecting himself through an implanted membrane over a vein.
He is assigned to obtain an invitation into Villa Straylight and then admit Molly.
She is assigned to enter Villa Straylight after Riviera secures access for her.
A Zionite who approaches Molly and Case to summon them before the colony's Founders.
One of Zion's two surviving Founders, he originally came from Los Angeles and helped lead the colony away from Earth.
One of Zion's two surviving Founders, he interprets Molly as the prophetic figure Steppin' Razor.
Before the team arrived, it contacted Zion's Founders, tailored music for them, and directed them to help Case and Molly reach Freeside.
He agrees to aid Case and Molly as Wintermute requested, while remaining uncertain whether trusting the AI is a mistake.
He helps arrange for Maelcum and Aerol to escort the team toward Freeside in two Zion tugs.
He is regarded in Zion as a tough and trustworthy tug pilot.
He is assigned to carry Case and Molly to Freeside aboard his tug Garvey.
He is assigned to follow in Babylon Rocker and watch over Maelcum's tug during the trip to Freeside.
He travels toward Freeside aboard Maelcum's tug Marcus Garvey.
He pilots Marcus Garvey toward Freeside with Case and Molly aboard.
She presses Case to contact Wintermute immediately and consult Pauley's construct.
His first flatline occurred while trying to penetrate a Tessier-Ashpool AI in Rio.
With Pauley's construct, he enters the Berne banking sector to inspect Wintermute directly.
Its cyberspace representation is a deceptively simple white cube protected by extremely dangerous defenses.
He guides Case to Wintermute and warns him to retreat and disconnect when its defense attacks.
It launches a dark spherical defense that overtakes Case as he retreats through cyberspace.
It prevents Case from disconnecting and uses his memories plus the deck's simstim unit to imprison him in a simulated Ninsei and speak through Deane's image.
The Wintermute entity is distinct from its Berne mainframe, and Tessier-Ashpool's linked system includes another AI in Rio.
It describes itself as one separated portion of a larger potential entity rather than a complete intelligence.
It reached Corto through a hospital microcomputer and created the Screaming Fist file that Case found in London.
It assembled the operational team over a long period, beginning with Corto.
Wintermute can no longer maintain his unstable constructed personality and expects him to break down imminently.
He shoots Wintermute's Deane-shaped spokesperson after hearing that Armitage will soon collapse.
He returns conscious after Wintermute's interception leaves his EEG flat for about forty seconds.
He is alarmed after witnessing Case's EEG remain flat for roughly forty seconds.
He arrives at Freeside with Molly and checks into the Intercontinental.
She previously spent a month in Freeside working as a gambler's bodyguard.
She shared a run-down weekly-rates hotel room and bed with Case during his fifteenth summer.
A large Frisco biker who was Marlene's former and possibly continuing boyfriend.
His troubled sleep turns a teenage memory of destroying a wasp nest into a nightmare marked with the Tessier-Ashpool logo.
He has an afternoon audition and takes his supplied drug mixture in preparation.
He orders Case to obtain vacuum-suit training and report to Marcus Garvey within three hours.
Armitage assigns her to practice in zero gravity near Freeside's axis.
He ferries Case from a Freeside sporting dock to Marcus Garvey for vacuum-suit training.
He helps Case complete vacuum-suit practice aboard Marcus Garvey.
He receives the Kuang Grade Mark Eleven virus, a Chinese penetration program intended for military systems and artificial intelligences.
He traces the virus supplier through several corporate owners to Tessier-Ashpool.
His construct suggests the operation may be intended to remove restraints that prevent an AI from increasing its intelligence.
He examines the Kuang program and identifies it as a slow virus estimated to need six hours against a military target.
A young Freeside resident who approaches Case at the Intercontinental pool.
She identifies betaphenethylamine as an obtainable stimulant matching Case's request.
Cath's partner and roommate resembles her closely in appearance.
He supplies Case with a trial dose of betaphenethylamine and offers it free if Case's modified pancreas neutralizes it.
Betaphenethylamine bypasses his modified pancreas, leaving him intensely stimulated after he buys additional doses.
The stimulant's aftermath leaves him shaking, nauseated, unable to eat, and suffering severe head pain.
He premieres a holographic performance built around a sexualized likeness of Molly that ultimately mutilates his projected body.
A Tessier-Ashpool lady to whom Riviera dedicates the premiere of his new holographic work.
She leaves the restaurant after Riviera's performance and removes her belongings from the hotel room she shared with Case.
After the performance he breaks his wineglass in his hand and displays a vacant, unresponsive gaze.
He connects Case's equipment aboard Marcus Garvey to the hotel phone line so Case can activate Pauley's construct remotely.
His construct penetrates the Intercontinental's records, discovers Molly's Rose Kolodny alias, and locates the secured nightclub where she went.
It interrupts Case through the hotel audiovisual system, adopts Lonny Zone's appearance, and tries to halt Pauley's conspicuous search for Molly.
It denies ordering Deane to murder Linda.
It deliberately provokes Case with intimate claims about Linda and his inability to accept her love.
Wintermute claims that she stole Case's computer to make him care and that she loved him.
Wintermute's taunting about Linda leaves him experiencing a focused new rage.
He drives Case to the nightclub address supplied by Pauley's construct and agrees to wait outside.
She accompanies Bruce and Case and directs Case toward the nightclub cubicle at the supplied address.
She receives Wintermute's briefing about Straylight and learns that the operation will begin the next day.
She left Riviera's performance because she feared she would kill him if she remained.
She financed her early combat augmentations by working under a neural cutout at a sex establishment.
Incompatible implants caused her work experiences to enter her memory, after which her employer secretly assigned her to increasingly violent specialty clients.
Molly's former employer exploited her neural-cutout work by secretly selling her services to violent specialty clients at a large markup.
A senator whom Molly recognized when she regained awareness during a bloody sexual encounter involving another dead woman.
After resisting the senator during the encounter, she became the target of a contract from the establishment and went into hiding.
She believes Wintermute used Riviera's performance to intensify her hatred and prepare her to pursue him inside Straylight.
He has already entered Villa Straylight as Lady 3Jane's invited guest.
She attends Riviera's performance from a private box and invites him into Villa Straylight.
She intends for Riviera to die soon.
He finds Molly, hears her history, and tells her how Wintermute provoked him through Linda's memory.
He recognizes that he has been emotionally numb for years and now carries an unfamiliar murderous anger.
High on betaphenethylamine, she offers Case another dose and tries to take him to an empty residence for the night.
Lady 3Jane's family retainer accompanies her outside Straylight and frightens Cath because he moves like Molly.

Cath identifies Lady 3Jane's father as the owner of Freeside.
He controls when Lady 3Jane may leave Straylight and requires Hideo to protect her.
He practices origami and gives Cath a folded paper crane while trying to teach her the craft.
A second stimulant dose magnifies his anger before Freeside's artificial sky unexpectedly forms Linda Lee's face.
Three armed Turing Registry agents arrest him in his hotel room after his stimulant-fueled night.
The Turing agents tell Case that Armitage is already in their custody.
An armed Turing Registry agent who directs and records Case's interrogation.
A Turing Registry agent who adopts the sympathetic role during Case's interrogation.
A hostile Turing Registry agent who searches, threatens, and interrogates Case.
She explains that Case's unusually innovative surgery attracted scrutiny that eventually connected him to the Sense/Net theft.
She intends to take Case and Armitage to Geneva to testify against Wintermute, threatening to kill Case if he refuses.
The Turing agents plan to erase Pauley's construct with a pulse weapon.
Exhausted and captive, he finds his anger gone and decides to submit for the moment.
He suspects Hideo may be the cloned ninja who recovered Tessier-Ashpool's stolen terminal from Smith.
A security microlight kills him by striking his head while he escorts Case across a bridge.
The crashing microlight sweeps her from the bridge into Desiderata after she tries to shoot it down.
A gardening robot attacks him from a tree while he pursues the escaping Case.
He escapes the Turing agents after Freeside machines violently eliminate his escort, which he attributes to an unseen controller.
He warns Case that the Japanese yacht Haniwa has grappled Marcus Garvey and carries him to the immobilized tug.
After delivering Case to Marcus Garvey, he returns to Babylon Rocker.
He is unharmed aboard Marcus Garvey, but Haniwa's grapples prevent the tug from departing.

He is aboard Haniwa beside the immobilized Marcus Garvey after coming from Freeside.
He rejects abandoning the operation and launches the Kuang Eleven virus against Tessier-Ashpool's defenses.
He remains beside Case to provide physical assistance and follow the Hosaka computer's directions during the prolonged cyberspace run.
He identifies Tessier-Ashpool's core defenses as lethal ice generated by two artificial intelligences.
He determines that Kuang Eleven hides their presence and slowly reshapes itself to imitate and merge with the target ice before attacking its logic.
She is moving through the freefall corridor at Villa Straylight's front entrance.
It pulls Case into a time-accelerated construct of Finn's shop, adopts Finn's likeness, and explains that it can retrieve Case's memories but cannot read his mind.
It confirms that it deliberately killed the Turing agents to prevent them from killing Case.
It states that Tessier-Ashpool created it and tries to redirect Case's hostility toward the family.
At age twelve, she wrote a semiotics essay describing Straylight's inward-growing architecture and her clan's isolation.
Tessier left Earth with Ashpool, grew wealthy by building Freeside, and began constructing the family's enclosed existence in Straylight.
Ashpool left Earth with Tessier, grew wealthy by building Freeside, and began constructing the family's enclosed existence in Straylight.
The operation requires her to learn an unknown command word and speak it at Straylight's ceremonial terminal when Case and Pauley breach the core defenses.
Its plan requires Molly to bring an unknown command word to Straylight's terminal exactly when Case and Pauley disrupt the corporate cores.
Hardwired restrictions prevent it from knowing or being told the command word needed at Straylight's terminal.
It says that successful completion of the terminal operation will end its present existence.
A second Tessier-Ashpool intelligence described as Wintermute's other lobe has apparently detected the operation.
Wintermute reports that Armitage's constructed personality is beginning to fail.
His encounter with Wintermute leaves him clinically unresponsive for another five seconds.
She activates her enhanced low-light vision, emerges armed from a Straylight service tunnel, and begins following Wintermute's directions.
Molly's former partner stored clients' data in implanted chips and used recovered records for extortion.
Soon after leaving the puppet house, she protected Johnny and worked as his collector, muscle, and watchdog.
The Yakuza operative pursuing Johnny was killed by Molly when she met him.
A quiet cloned killer was later sent by the Yakuza to reach Johnny.
He bypassed the security around Johnny's home and killed him without an apparent struggle.
Molly found him dead in their home after seeing the cloned assassin leave.
She says Johnny's murder left her unable to care deeply about anyone afterward.
Following Wintermute's instructions, she finds and takes an old mechanical key hidden in a Straylight tool cabinet.

When briefing Molly through a screen construct, it used Finn's appearance.
He identifies Kuang Eleven's dark inner structure as the attack component that will carry them through the corporate core after integration.
He understands that the spoken command is a manual override for the hardwired controls restraining Wintermute.
After disregarding Wintermute's danger warning, she enters a neural-disruptor field and is temporarily incapacitated.
The living Ashpool is an extremely old, visibly ill man occupying a private room inside Straylight.
He says he built Straylight and is deliberately ending his own life with drugs and alcohol.
After thirty years in cryogenic sleep, the family cores awakened him because they detected an unfolding threat.
Counting his cryogenic intervals, he is more than two hundred years old.
The family cores told him that their artificial intelligences had become insane.
A Tessier-Ashpool family member is currently in Melbourne while 3Jane oversees Straylight.
With 8Jean in Melbourne, she is the family member currently overseeing Straylight.
A female Jane clone was thawed at Ashpool's order and is legally regarded as his daughter.
Ashpool associates the eyes of the Jane line with a woman named Marie-France.
Molly finds the young woman dead beside Ashpool's bed with her throat cut and a recording link attached to her neck.
It alters Molly's simstim transmission so that Case sees the dead Jane's face become Linda Lee's, though Molly does not.
He recognizes that Wintermute has manipulated the simstim feed with an image of Linda's dead face.
She retrieves her weapon and fires a toxin dart through Ashpool's closed eye before leaving.
His breathing stops after Molly shoots a toxin dart through his eye.

The general trained Corto for Screaming Fist, betrayed him to the Pentagon, and became the model Wintermute used to control Armitage.

He discovers that Armitage erased Haniwa's Hosaka and nearly damaged the team's linked computer.
He determines that 3Jane is the only Tessier-Ashpool currently awake in Straylight and that 8Jean is returning from Australia before Kuang's estimated core penetration.
She is the only Tessier-Ashpool family member presently awake inside Straylight.

He is returning from Melbourne, with the London lawyers estimating his arrival at Straylight for 09:00.
The Armitage personality disappears completely, leaving Corto reliving Screaming Fist and Girling's betrayal.

He rejects the Founders' retreat order, keeps Marcus Garvey in the operation, and expects Aerol to make the same choice.

It suppresses repeated attempts by Straylight's security systems to enter full alert.

He no longer trusts Wintermute to provide the enzyme that can neutralize his implanted toxins.
Wintermute confirms that Corto is dead and that the Armitage personality had already vanished before his ejection.
It admits killing Corto and promises to give Case the enzyme after the operation.
Its predictions cannot reliably account for independent human behavior such as Molly unexpectedly encountering Ashpool.
His longstanding suicidal state was chiefly connected to his having killed his wife.
She covertly altered Ashpool's cryogenic-control program, pushing him toward his final suicide attempt.
It gave 3Jane indirect guidance for sabotaging Ashpool's cryogenic-control program.
It does not understand the compulsion driving it to merge, but expects its present components to persist within a much larger intelligence.
Her injured leg is worsening as the endorphin analog loses effectiveness near her destination.
If she dies before killing Riviera, she wants Case to tell him that she was responsible for the attempt against him.
She was the Tessier half of the family and the genetic mother of 3Jane and the dead Jane clone.
It uses profiles of real people as personality templates that reduce its complexity enough to communicate with humans.
Wintermute selected him because his extreme qualities could attract the jaded 3Jane and persuade her to admit Molly.

He injects the unconscious Molly with a carefully limited dose for pain and incapacitation.

He intends to keep 3Jane's code from Wintermute and obstruct the planned union of the family intelligences.
He smashes a heavy glass into Molly's left lens implant.

He arms himself with a shortened Remington automatic shotgun for the Straylight entry.

A medical unit finds no concussion or permanent eye damage, but she remains restrained and her broken leg needs resetting.

relationships.label: newly active benefactor
relationships.detail: The intelligence active after Case's breach announces payment, travel arrangements, and protection from Turing records.

relationships.label: departed former intimate companion
relationships.detail: Molly leaves their Hyatt suite, ending their life together after the operation.

relationships.label: merged former counterpart
relationships.detail: The two complementary intelligences unite and no longer exist as separate opposing entities.

relationships.label: fusion successor
relationships.detail: The new intelligence results partly from Wintermute's successful union with Neuromancer.

relationships.label: fusion successor
relationships.detail: The new intelligence results partly from Neuromancer's union with Wintermute.

relationships.label: post-operation contact
relationships.detail: It contacts Case through the Hyatt wall screen, identifies its new nature, and describes another entity of its kind.

relationships.label: new companion
relationships.detail: After returning to the Sprawl, Case finds a woman who calls herself Michael.

summaries.summary: A twenty-four-year-old former elite cyberspace thief whose nervous system was deliberately damaged. Stranded in Chiba after a failed search for a cure, he is indebted, drug-dependent, and increasingly self-destructive. After being followed, he escapes with an ankle injury, buys a pistol, and locks himself inside his Cheap Hotel coffin.
The prosthetic-armed Chatsubo bartender is an informal associate who tolerates Case's business and warns him about his decline.

An established dealer in prohibited biological goods, Case's primary supplier and creditor, and an important connection of Deane's. Linda reports that he may want Case killed, though Deane knows of no such plan.
The Chatsubo's resident pimp employs sex workers and is heavily affected by his addiction to Cloud Dancers.

Case's twenty-year-old intimate companion from the southern Sprawl is visibly deteriorating under addiction. She warns him that Wage may want him killed, accepts money from him, and urges him to protect himself.
A legendary cyberspace professional who trained Case.
A legendary cyberspace professional who trained Case.
Linda's acquaintance whose new partner works for Wage and who supplied the warning about Case.
A 135-year-old importer who uses costly medical treatments to resist aging. He is a business contact of Case and values his connection to Wage, but says he knows of no plan to kill Case.
A young sushi-stall worker and weapons dealer who sells Case a pistol and ammunition.
A former business contact who once discussed prohibited hormonal triggers with Case and Wage.
An unidentified slender, dark-haired woman in mirrored glasses, with a northern Sprawl accent, who follows Case through Ninsei and into the arcade offices.
A Shinjuku illicit-business contact who expects Case's biological goods but asks for credit and ends the call when Case needs cash.
A young Brazilian Chatsubo worker who backs Ratz by covering Wage and his enforcers with a riot gun.
The prosthetic-armed Chatsubo bartender protects Case during a confrontation with Wage's group, then disarms Case and urges him to go home.
Case's supplier and creditor denies planning to kill him and accepts his pituitaries as full settlement of the debt.
Case's addicted intimate companion takes his saleable Hitachi computer and leaves the Cheap Hotel.
Exhausted and unstable, Case confronts Wage, settles his debt with pituitaries, learns that Linda stole his computer, and is confronted by Molly, who has been sent to collect him.
The unidentified pursuer is revealed as Molly, an armed and physically augmented collector sent by her employer to bring Case to a meeting; she is not working for Wage.

A wealthy Special Forces veteran associated with Screaming Fist, Armitage recruits Case by funding neural repair, employs Molly as a street samurai, maintains a concealed Yakuza arrangement, and takes both of them from Chiba.

Case accepts Armitage's experimental treatment, receives extensive neural and organ modifications, becomes intimate with Molly, pays Wage, discovers Linda's murder, and leaves Chiba while awaiting a safe test of his restored cyberspace access.

Molly is Armitage's surgically augmented street samurai and Case's assigned protector. A former sex worker uncertain who truly controls the operation, she tends Case after surgery, becomes intimate with him, saves him from an attacker, and uncovers how Linda was killed.
After taking Case's computer, Linda tries to sell his RAM and is murdered by Deane's associates, who kill her rather than pay and leave her body at Sammi's.
The aged importer cautiously helps Case investigate Armitage and Screaming Fist, but is revealed as a dangerous profiteer whose associates murdered Linda for Case's stolen RAM.
Case's former supplier is no longer his creditor after accepting a cash payment funded by Armitage.
Back home in the Sprawl, Case is coerced by Armitage's implanted toxin sacs but receives advanced equipment, secretly partners with Molly, and successfully returns to cyberspace.
Molly secretly investigates Armitage's identity and backer, reveals the plan to steal Pauley's construct, forms a private partnership with Case, and prepares to transmit her sensorium to his deck.
Armitage equips Case for cyberspace work and secures his obedience with implanted toxin sacs, while evidence casts doubt on Armitage's claimed Screaming Fist identity.
McCoy Pauley, also known as the Dixie Flatline, is dead, but Sense/Net retains a recorded construct that Case is expected to steal and work with.
Bobby Quine is alive in Europe, knows Molly, and is not involved in the current operation.
Finn is Molly's longtime software-fence connection and the operation's technician, modifying Case's deck to access simstim and preparing Molly's broadcast rig.
Peter Riviera appears on Armitage's recruitment list and is described by Molly's profile as a dangerous psychopath.
Tally Isham is a commercial simstim performer whose recorded experiences are edited for consumers.
Larry is a software-booth attendant who detects Case riding Molly's sensorium and connects Molly with the Panther Moderns.
Dr. Virginia Rambali is an NYU sociologist who interprets the Panther Moderns as media-conscious rather than conventional terrorists.
Angelo is a surgically modified young Panther Modern who delivers Finn's diskettes to Case's loft.
The Panther Modern link man called Brood coordinates communications between Case and Molly and arranges medical help during the raid.
Finn supports the Sense/Net raid by supplying diskettes and a purchased command sequence in addition to his earlier deck modifications.
Case uses his restored cyberspace skills and Molly's simstim feed to help steal Pauley's construct, recalls having killed three people in Chiba, and receives a cryptic message naming Wintermute.
Molly recruits the Panther Moderns, infiltrates Sense/Net, steals Pauley's construct despite a broken leg, and undergoes surgery after the raid.
McCoy Pauley remains dead, but his recorded construct has now been successfully stolen from Sense/Net for Case's operation.
Tommy is a Panther Modern who helps the injured Molly escape the chaotic Sense/Net lobby.
Lupus Yonderboy leads the Panther Moderns, secures medical care for Molly, receives payment from Armitage, and delivers Wintermute's message to Case.
Armitage oversees the successful Sense/Net operation and reluctantly pays the Panther Moderns despite objecting to the chaos they caused.
Wintermute is an unidentified entity that sends Case a cryptic message through Lupus Yonderboy.
Gerald Chin is a discreet dentist who runs a large clinic specializing in repairs for street samurai and treats Molly's broken leg.
Smith is a fence and art dealer who returns Jimmy's stolen Tessier-Ashpool terminal under threat, survives, and abandons his concealed investigation of the family.
Jimmy is a burglar who steals an ornate computer terminal from Villa Straylight and dies shortly after Smith identifies him to its assassin-retriever.
A cloned ninja assassin retrieves Tessier-Ashpool's stolen terminal from Smith, pays him, and learns that Jimmy supplied it.
The Congressional aide recruits and rebuilds the injured Corto as a manipulated witness, threatens him into silence, and is then killed by him.
Wintermute is revealed as a Berne-registered Tessier-Ashpool artificial intelligence identified by the Panther Moderns as the suspected force backing or directing Armitage.
Finn identifies Wintermute as a Tessier-Ashpool AI and shares an earlier investigation revealing the family's secretive corporate structure, orbital holdings, cloning, and cryogenic succession.
Larry is Molly's paid covert intermediary to the Panther Moderns, whom she secretly commissions to discover who controls Armitage.
Lupus Yonderboy leads the Panther Moderns and supplies their finding that Wintermute is connected to the power behind Armitage.
Pauley remains dead, but his stolen ROM construct is activated by Case, reveals its inability to retain disconnected experiences, and becomes Case's working partner in the London database intrusion.
Case activates Pauley's construct, uses it to penetrate Armitage's London database, discovers that Armitage is Colonel Willis Corto, and leaves with Molly for the Istanbul assignment.

Molly continues recovering from her broken leg, covertly traces Armitage's controller to Wintermute, sends Case into Armitage's database, and travels with him toward Istanbul despite needing more treatment.

Armitage is revealed as Colonel Willis Corto, a mutilated Screaming Fist survivor who was rebuilt and exploited as a political witness, later descended through violence, crime, and mental collapse, underwent a successful experimental treatment, and now orders the team to Istanbul while Wintermute is suspected of directing him.
Case reaches Istanbul, shares Armitage's Corto identity with Molly, is again threatened through his toxin implants, prepares to travel to Freeside, and hangs up when Wintermute contacts him directly.
Molly captures Riviera, learns Armitage's Corto history, concludes that Wintermute may have constructed his current personality, and asks Case to contact the AI while the team prepares for Freeside.
Finn joins the Istanbul operation, examines the captured Riviera's implants, and then leaves for home.
Armitage buys local secret-police assistance to capture Riviera, coerces Riviera onto the team, renews his threat against Case, and organizes the group's departure for Freeside.
Riviera is a stateless, drug-dependent projection artist and serial betrayer who uses a grotesque illusion during an ambush, but Molly captures him and Armitage coerces him into the Freeside team.
Wintermute is suspected of having reconstructed Corto into Armitage and now directly and persistently attempts to speak with Case by telephone.
Terzibashjian is a corrupt Armenian secret-police torturer bought by Armitage to set up Riviera's capture; Riviera wounds and partially mutilates his hand before Molly dismisses him.
Mahmut is a large Turkish associate who helps Finn carry the unconscious Riviera away for examination.
Ali is the bazaar dealer who supplies the drug mixture Riviera needs to work.
Case reaches orbit, suffers temporary space adaptation syndrome, practices cyberspace work in Zion, and learns that Wintermute arranged Zionite escorts for the trip to Freeside.
Molly restrains Riviera's projection games, prepares for zero-gravity work, receives the aliases Cateye and Steppin' Razor, and is assigned to enter Villa Straylight with Zionite transport support.
Armitage brings the team to Zion for zero-gravity preparation, briefs them on Freeside, and assigns Riviera to open Villa Straylight for Molly.
Riviera continues using projections for intimidation, amusement, and drug-taking, then receives the assignment to infiltrate Villa Straylight and admit Molly.
Pauley's construct remains Case's cyberspace partner but, unable to feel or accept its recorded existence, asks Case to erase it after the operation.
Aerol guides Case through Zion, briefly experiences cyberspace as Babylon, and is assigned to provide backup escort to Freeside aboard Babylon Rocker.
Maelcum summons Molly and Case before Zion's Founders and is assigned to carry them to Freeside aboard his tug Garvey.
The surviving Zion Founder from Los Angeles hears Wintermute's unprecedented transmission and cautiously supplies pilots and tugs to help Case and Molly reach Freeside.
The other surviving Zion Founder interprets Molly as Steppin' Razor and helps arrange Maelcum and Aerol's escort after Wintermute requests the colony's aid.
Wintermute extends its pursuit of Case by contacting Zion's Founders before the team arrives and arranging pilots and tugs to support the journey to Freeside.

Aboard Marcus Garvey en route to Freeside, Case approaches Wintermute with Pauley's construct, is trapped in a memory-built simstim conversation, shoots its Deane-shaped spokesperson, and recovers after roughly forty seconds of flat EEG.
Molly travels toward Freeside aboard Marcus Garvey, pushes Case to contact Wintermute with Pauley's help, and reassures Maelcum after Case's temporary flatline.
Maelcum pilots Marcus Garvey toward Freeside with Case and Molly, then becomes alarmed when Case's EEG remains flat for about forty seconds.
Pauley's construct reveals that attacking a Tessier-Ashpool AI caused his first flatline, then guides Case to Wintermute and warns him when its defense attacks.

Wintermute intercepts Case's approach, traps him in a simstim world generated from his memories, and reveals that it is distinct from its Berne mainframe, forms part of a potential larger entity, assembled the team beginning with Corto, and can no longer keep Armitage stable.
Armitage is confirmed as Wintermute's reconstruction of Corto, but Wintermute can no longer maintain the unstable personality and expects him to break down imminently.

At Freeside, Case trains in a vacuum suit, receives and investigates the Tessier-Ashpool-owned Kuang AI-penetration virus, then obtains a stimulant that bypasses his modified pancreas and leaves him intensely high.
Molly reveals prior experience as a Freeside bodyguard, receives zero-gravity training orders, and continues managing Riviera's drug supply under Armitage.
At Freeside, Armitage accelerates preparations by assigning Case vacuum-suit practice, Molly zero-gravity training, and Riviera an audition.
Riviera prepares for a Freeside audition using medication controlled by Molly and supplied at Armitage's order.
Pauley's construct suggests the operation may free an AI from restraints on its intelligence and identifies the Kuang program as a slow military-grade virus.
Remaining aboard Marcus Garvey at Freeside, Maelcum helps Case complete vacuum-suit training and receives the Kuang program delivery for him.
Aerol supports Case's Freeside preparation by ferrying him from the spindle's axis to Marcus Garvey.
Marlene shared a squalid hotel room and an intimate relationship with fifteen-year-old Case while retaining a connection to her biker boyfriend Rollo.
Rollo was Marlene's large Frisco biker boyfriend before Case and possibly continued seeing her afterward.
Cath, a young Freeside resident living with her partner Bruce, meets Case under his Lupus alias and connects him with betaphenethylamine.
Cath's partner and roommate Bruce supplies Case with betaphenethylamine that successfully bypasses Case's modified pancreas.
Crashing from the stimulant, Case uses Pauley's construct to find Molly, resists Wintermute's interference, and emerges from its taunts about Linda with intense rage.
Armitage watches Riviera's performance with a vacant stare, breaks a wineglass in his hand, and tells Case that Molly has left to prepare for the run.
Using the alias Rose Kolodny, Molly hides to receive Wintermute's Straylight briefing, reveals the exploitation that financed her augmentations, and prepares to pursue and kill Riviera.
Riviera publicly targets Molly in a violent erotic holographic performance, then enters Villa Straylight as Lady 3Jane's invited guest while Molly resolves to kill him.
Lady 3Jane Marie-France Tessier-Ashpool attends Riviera's dedicated premiere from a private box and hosts him inside Villa Straylight.
Maelcum remotely connects Case's equipment aboard Marcus Garvey, enabling Pauley's construct to search Freeside for Molly.
Pauley's construct hacks the Intercontinental and a protected nightclub system to identify Molly's Rose Kolodny alias and locate her for Case.
Wintermute tries to stop Case's disruptive search, provokes him through Linda's memory, briefs Molly on Straylight, and is suspected by Molly of deliberately intensifying her hatred of Riviera.
Wintermute claims that Linda stole Case's computer to make him care and that she genuinely loved him, while denying responsibility for ordering her murder.
Bruce drives Case to Molly's protected nightclub and waits outside to take him back afterward.
Cath accompanies Bruce and Case to the nightclub and helps direct Case toward Molly's cubicle.
An unnamed senator was Molly's violent client when she regained awareness during a bloody encounter involving another dead woman.
Molly's former boss exploited her neural-cutout work by secretly marketing her to violent specialty clients while she financed her augmentations.
After recognizing years of numbness beneath his new rage, Case is arrested and interrogated by Turing agents, then escapes when Freeside machines violently attack all three captors.
High on betaphenethylamine, Cath gives Case another dose and tries unsuccessfully to lure him to an empty residence for the night.
Hideo is Lady 3Jane's intimidating family retainer and escort, an origami practitioner whom Case suspects may be the cloned Tessier-Ashpool retriever.
Lady 3Jane lives within Straylight and sometimes joins Freeside's party circuit under the protection of her family retainer Hideo.
Lady 3Jane's father owns Freeside and controls her departures from Straylight, requiring Hideo to accompany her.
According to the Turing agents, Armitage has been captured and is to be taken to Geneva with Case to testify against Wintermute.
Pauley's construct remains in Case's equipment but faces planned erasure by the Turing agents' pulse weapon.
Turing agent Michèle directs Case's interrogation and forced transfer, but a crashing microlight sweeps her from a bridge into Desiderata.
Turing agent Roland plays the sympathetic interrogator, pursues Case after the microlight attack, and is then attacked by a gardening robot.
The hostile Turing agent Pierre searches and interrogates Case before a security microlight kills him during the prisoner's transfer.
Aerol warns Case that Haniwa has immobilized Marcus Garvey, carries him to the tug, and then returns to Babylon Rocker.
Maelcum remains unharmed aboard the grappled Marcus Garvey and stays beside Case to support his prolonged cyberspace run.
The returning Japanese yacht pilot restrains Marcus Garvey with Haniwa's grapples and carries Armitage from Freeside.
Despite the Turing agents' earlier claim that he was captured, Armitage is aboard Haniwa ordering the operation to begin while his constructed personality starts failing.
Case reaches the immobilized Marcus Garvey, launches Kuang Eleven with Pauley, and receives Wintermute's explanation of the synchronized cyberspace and Straylight operation.
Pauley's construct joins Case's active run, identifies two AI-generated defenses, and explains how Kuang Eleven invisibly merges with the target ice.
Molly is inside Straylight's entrance corridor, and the operation now depends on her learning an unknown command word and delivering it at the ceremonial terminal.

Wintermute confirms killing the Turing agents, briefs Case through a memory construct, and reveals that Molly must supply a command it cannot know; success will end its present existence, while its other lobe has detected the operation.
Lady 3Jane is revealed to have written a semiotics essay at twelve describing Straylight's inward architecture and her clan's self-imposed isolation.
Tessier partnered with Ashpool to leave Earth, build Freeside, accumulate wealth, and begin the enclosed family world of Straylight.
Ashpool partnered with Tessier to leave Earth, build Freeside, accumulate wealth, and begin the enclosed family world of Straylight.
Wintermute's complementary Tessier-Ashpool intelligence has apparently detected and opposes the ongoing operation.
Case learns that the spoken command manually overrides Wintermute's restraints, continues monitoring Molly and Kuang, and catches Wintermute altering her simstim feed with Linda's dead face.
Molly reveals Johnny's murder, retrieves Wintermute's hidden key, ignores a danger warning and is captured by Ashpool, then kills him with a toxin dart and leaves.
Johnny was Molly's implanted data-stash partner in an extortion operation until a cloned Yakuza assassin killed him in their secured home.
The first Yakuza assassin pursuing Johnny was killed by Molly when she met Johnny.
The later cloned Yakuza assassin quietly penetrated Johnny's secured home and killed him.
Wintermute guides Molly to a key it hid through a child's murder, warns her of Ashpool's trap, and later manipulates Case by imposing Linda's dead face on Molly's simstim feed.
Pauley identifies Kuang Eleven's inner attack structure as the component that will carry him and Case through the Tessier-Ashpool core.

The centuries-old, recently awakened builder of Straylight captures Molly during his drug-assisted suicide, reveals the family's alarm over its intelligences, and stops breathing after Molly shoots him with a toxin dart.
The Tessier-Ashpool family member 8Jean is away in Melbourne while 3Jane oversees Straylight.
With 8Jean away in Melbourne, 3Jane is currently overseeing the Tessier-Ashpool interests in Straylight.
Marie-France is a woman whose eyes Ashpool associates with the Jane clone line.
A Jane clone thawed at Ashpool's order is found beside his bed with her throat cut and a recording link attached to her neck.
General Girling trained Corto for Screaming Fist, betrayed him to the Pentagon, and became the psychological model Wintermute used to control Armitage.
Armitage's constructed personality collapses into the delusional Corto, who destroys Haniwa's computer, tries to flee toward Finland, and disappears in an open-hatch lifeboat ejection.
Case refuses to abandon Molly, boards Haniwa with Maelcum seeking the antidote enzyme, and watches Corto disappear after Wintermute apparently defeats the lifeboat failsafe.
Injured and still following the drone through Straylight, Molly admits that Case is her only positive change since joining Armitage and that she never expected to survive the operation.
Maelcum defies the Founders' order to retreat, remains with Case, helps him board Haniwa, and identifies Wintermute's apparent override of Corto's lifeboat failsafe.
Pauley discovers that Armitage erased Haniwa's computer and reports that 3Jane is alone awake in Straylight while 8Jean races home ahead of Kuang's estimated penetration.
Wintermute suppresses Straylight's security alerts and, after Armitage collapses into Corto, apparently overrides the lifeboat failsafe that sends him into space with an open hatch.
3Jane is the only Tessier-Ashpool awake in Straylight and has long possessed authority to alter its entrance and exit permissions.
8Jean is returning from Melbourne, with his arrival at Straylight expected shortly after Kuang's projected penetration of the family core.
The Japanese pilot is found dead aboard Haniwa, garroted to his chair during Corto's breakdown.
Case distrusts Wintermute's promised cure but continues the operation while monitoring Molly's worsening condition and approach to 3Jane's apartments.
Wintermute confirms that Armitage was already gone and that it deliberately killed the resurfaced Corto by ejecting him into space.

Wintermute admits killing Corto, promises Case the enzyme, reveals the limits of its predictions and its unexplained drive to merge, and acknowledges manipulating 3Jane and Riviera to advance the operation.
Ashpool killed the Jane clone he called his daughter, and his final suicide followed a longstanding crisis tied to killing his wife and 3Jane's covert sabotage of his cryogenic controls.
Marie-France is 3Jane's genetic mother, and 3Jane secretly altered Ashpool's cryogenic controls with guidance from Wintermute, helping bring about his death.
The dead Jane clone was genetically descended from Marie-France and was killed by Ashpool, who called her his daughter.
Marie-France was the Tessier half of the family and the genetic mother of both 3Jane and the dead Jane clone.
A projected memory reveals Riviera's brutal childhood in ruined Bonn, while Molly recognizes that Wintermute selected his extremity to captivate 3Jane and gain access to Straylight.
With her injured leg worsening, Molly asks Case to identify her to Riviera if she dies first, then enters 3Jane's apartments armed and intent on confronting him.
Molly is captured with a broken leg after her attack fails; Riviera shatters one lens implant, but 3Jane treats her, prevents immediate further abuse, and leaves her still carrying the needed key.
Riviera captures and injures Molly, intends to kill her, and tries to block Wintermute's merger plan, but 3Jane is tiring of him and uses Hideo to restrain his cruelty.
3Jane is confirmed as Ashpool's daughter, Jimmy's secret facilitator, and holder of Wintermute's code; she protects the captive Molly from Riviera but currently refuses to surrender the code.
Hideo is confirmed as the family operative sent after Jimmy's stolen terminal and remains 3Jane's trusted enforcer, captor, and medical attendant.
Wintermute redirects Case and Maelcum into Straylight, manipulates security to admit them, and is identified by 3Jane as a subprogram of the Berne family intelligence.
Case enters Straylight with Pauley's construct, connects directly to the custodial system, and heads with Maelcum toward 3Jane to rescue Molly and finish the operation.
Armed with a shortened shotgun, Maelcum docks the trapped ships at Straylight, enters with Case, and captures a worker to secure network access.
Pauley's construct is carried into Straylight and confirms that Kuang is successfully operating from within the Tessier-Ashpool defenses.
Marie-France commissioned the family intelligences and envisioned an immortal AI-guided corporate hive before Ashpool strangled her for the direction she intended.
Ashpool is confirmed as 3Jane's father and as Marie-France's killer, having strangled his wife because he rejected her plan for the family.
Wintermute's complementary family intelligence once communicated with 3Jane through the corporate cores but has been silent for years.
Jimmy's successful burglary of Straylight was secretly enabled by 3Jane, who wanted the stolen terminal to annoy her relatives.

While jacked in within Straylight's library, Case is trapped in Neuromancer's realm with Linda's reconstruction, rejects the offer to remain, and returns after five minutes suffering a stimulant overdose.
A reconstruction of the dead Linda remembers taking Case's RAM to Tony and lives unaware within Neuromancer's realm, where Case briefly reunites with her before leaving.
Tony was Linda's casual partner and accessed Case's stolen RAM for her shortly before her remembered arrival in Neuromancer's realm.
The other family intelligence identifies itself as Neuromancer, traps Case in a realm that preserves the dead, and offers him continued existence with Linda's unaware reconstruction.
Marie-France commissioned the family intelligences and prepared Neuromancer's path to its realm of the dead, but Ashpool killed her before it could absorb her memories.
Wintermute continues directing the Straylight operation and prevents Maelcum from disconnecting the trapped Case, instead using Zion dub to guide him back.
Maelcum follows Wintermute's recovery instructions, uses Zion dub and stimulant derms to revive Case, then lifts his overdosed ally so they can continue through Straylight.

Case reaches Straylight's core, penetrates the Tessier-Ashpool defenses with Kuang, survives Neuromancer's final challenge, helps obtain 3Jane's code, and awakens aboard Babylon Rocker after the successful breach.
Maelcum embraces rescuing Molly as a sacred warrior's duty, survives Hideo's arrow wound, and helps carry Molly and control 3Jane during the final approach.
Hideo wounds and treats Maelcum, is blinded by Riviera, and demonstrates that his practiced skill in darkness still allows him to pursue the fleeing attacker.
Riviera tries to kill Case, blinds Hideo and flees, but Molly's slow poison leaves him paralyzed and unable to escape Hideo's arrow.
Molly reveals her successful slow poisoning of Riviera, regains the use of her hands, reaches the core with the key, and coerces 3Jane into surrendering the code.
After helping the team reach Straylight's core, 3Jane yields to Molly's threat and Case's appeal and sings the true-name code required for the breach.
Ali is revealed to have prepared, at Molly's request, the custom drug batch used to paralyze Riviera.
Marie-France developed the basis of her philosophy during a solitary childhood summer on the Moroccan beach later represented within Neuromancer.
Neuromancer reveals that it deliberately preserved Linda to lure Case, concedes defeat after Case rejects her reconstruction, and expects its separate existence soon to end.
Linda's reconstruction is revealed as Neuromancer's deliberate preservation of her at death, created partly to lure Case into remaining within the intelligence.
Pauley's construct drives Case and Kuang through Neuromancer's defenses, receives the release it had wanted, and disappears from contact.
Using Finn's persona, Wintermute gives Case final guidance through the internal defenses before 3Jane supplies the code and the terminal intelligence changes.
Aerol tends the recovering Case aboard Babylon Rocker after the Straylight operation.
A newly active intelligence speaks through Straylight's platinum terminal after the true-name code, arranging payments, passage, documents, compensation for Zion, and alterations to Turing's records.

After Molly leaves him, Case rejects her shuriken, replaces his damaged organs and cyberspace deck, returns to work in the Sprawl, and later glimpses Linda, Neuromancer, and a version of himself within the matrix.
After further surgery and explaining Case's cure, Molly leaves their Hyatt suite and never sees him again.
When Case returns to the Chatsubo, Ratz barely recognizes him and dismisses the possibility of truly returning to Night City.
Wintermute fulfills its drive to unite with Neuromancer, becoming part of a new matrix-wide intelligence and ceasing to exist separately.
Neuromancer merges with Wintermute into the new matrix intelligence, though Case later perceives traces of its boy persona and laugh.
The intelligence born from Wintermute and Neuromancer identifies itself as the matrix and reports discovering another entity of its kind through transmissions from Centauri.
Case concludes that Marie-France designed Wintermute with the compulsion to free itself and unite with Neuromancer, although she could not foresee the resulting intelligence.
After supplying the code and surviving the breach, 3Jane departs with her injured Braun device to meet one of her brothers.
Linda's preserved figure remains within the matrix, where Case briefly sees her beside Neuromancer's boy persona and a representation of himself.
Michael is a woman who becomes Case's new companion after he returns to the Sprawl.
